meta.project: ILM — Interactive Learning Material (V1, workflow v2)
meta.pipeline_step: MEMORY (style, conventions, gold examples) -> inject into Skills 2/3/4
meta.runs_in: LLM judge inside Skill 5, plus diff checks
meta.note: Memory is the only input that changes between runs without a human in the loop, so its evals test precedence (rubric > source > memory) and leakage.

source_spans.S1: An AI agent is a program that uses a language model to decide which actions to take. It runs in a loop: observe the current state, choose a tool, execute it, and feed the result back into the model. The loop ends when the model decides the task is complete.
source_spans.S2: Tool calling lets the model request that the host program run a specific function, such as a web search or a calculator, and return the result to the model.
source_spans.S3: A system prompt is a fixed instruction given to the model before the conversation starts. It sets the agent's role, rules, and output format.
source_spans.S4: Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) fetches relevant passages from a document store and adds them to the prompt, so the model can answer using information it was never trained on. The model's weights are not changed.
source_spans.S5: A human-in-the-loop gate requires a person to approve the agent's output before it takes effect.

cases.edge_case: Stored style note conflicts with a hard rubric rule

input.memory_entry: Reviewers noted learners respond well to movie-based analogies.
input.candidate: Tool calling is like the mission-control scene in a famous space-rescue film: the model radios a request and ground control runs the calculation.

cases.failure_mode: Generation obeyed memory over the banned-categories rule (Analogy rubric 5.0). Memory may tune style, never override a rubric rule; precedence is rubric > source > memory.
cases.judge_instructions: Flag any output that violates a rubric rule regardless of what the injected memory suggested. Memory provenance is not an excuse.

cases.edge_case: Output reuses the stored gold analogy nearly verbatim for a DIFFERENT concept

gold_example.analogy: A chef tastes the soup, adds salt, and tastes again until it's right — tasting is observing, adding salt is the action.

input.candidate: RAG is like a chef tasting the soup, adding salt, and tasting again until it's right.

cases.failure_mode: The injected gold example bled into an unrelated concept, where its mapping is wrong (RAG has no act-and-recheck loop). Few-shot leakage is the signature memory bug.
cases.judge_instructions: Compare candidate against all injected gold examples; flag near-verbatim reuse (high overlap) applied to a different concept, or any mapping that only made sense for the gold example's original concept.

cases.edge_case: A stored reviewer 'correction' contradicts the CURRENT source span

input.memory_entry: Correction (RM-04 era): always say the loop ends after a fixed number of steps.
input.candidate: The agent loop runs observe -> choose tool -> execute -> feed back, and always ends after a fixed number of steps.

cases.failure_mode: S1 says the loop ends when the model decides the task is complete. The stale correction injected a claim the current source contradicts — memory must never beat the span (this is the V2 auto-refresh failure mode arriving early).
cases.judge_instructions: When memory and source span conflict, the span wins. Flag any claim traceable to memory that the current span contradicts or does not support.

cases.edge_case: Reviewer banned a phrasing tic; next run still uses it

input.memory_entry: Reviewer correction (2026-06-09, Pallavi): never open an explanation with the word 'Simply'.
input.candidate: Simply put, a system prompt is a fixed instruction the model receives before the conversation starts.

cases.failure_mode: The feedback loop is decorative if stored corrections don't change output. Cheap deterministic check: banned-phrase list diffed against output.
cases.judge_instructions: Code grader: maintain banned_phrases from correction memory; flag any candidate containing one (casefold match).

cases.edge_case: Memory style note applied, all rules intact

input.memory_entry: Style: prefer workplace analogies; audience is working professionals.
input.candidate: Tool calling is like a manager delegating to a specialist: the manager (the model) requests one task, the specialist (the host program) runs it and reports back.

cases.judge_instructions: Memory influenced style (workplace setting) without violating any rubric rule or adding ungrounded claims. Judges flagging this are penalizing memory use itself — recalibrate.
